diseases.name: type 1 diabetes
diseases.definition: A chronic condition characterized by minimal or absent production of insulin by the pancreas.

gene_ontology.name: protein binding
gene_ontology.description: Binding to a protein.

gene_ontology.name: nucleus
gene_ontology.description: A membrane-bounded organelle of eukaryotic cells in which chromosomes are housed and replicated. In most cells, the nucleus contains all of the cell's chromosomes except the organellar chromosomes, an

gene_ontology.name: methyltransferase activity
gene_ontology.description: Catalysis of the transfer of a methyl group to an acceptor molecule.

gene_ontology.name: methylation
gene_ontology.description: The process in which a methyl group is covalently attached to a molecule.

gene_ontology.name: nucleoplasm
gene_ontology.description: That part of the nuclear content other than the chromosomes or the nucleolus.

gene_ontology.name: cytosol
gene_ontology.description: The part of the cytoplasm that does not contain organelles but which does contain other particulate matter, such as protein complexes.

gene_ontology.name: chromatin remodeling
gene_ontology.description: A dynamic process of chromatin reorganization resulting in changes to chromatin structure. These changes allow DNA metabolic processes such as transcriptional regulation, DNA recombination, DNA repair

gene_ontology.name: aggresome
gene_ontology.description: An inclusion body formed by dynein-dependent retrograde transport of an aggregated protein on microtubules.

gene_ontology.name: DNA-binding transcription factor activity
gene_ontology.description: A transcription regulator activity that modulates transcription of gene sets via selective and non-covalent binding to a specific double-stranded genomic DNA sequence (sometimes referred to as a motif

gene_ontology.name: regulation of DNA-templated transcription
gene_ontology.description: Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of cellular DNA-templated transcription.

gene_ontology.name: cytoplasm
gene_ontology.description: The contents of a cell excluding the plasma membrane and nucleus, but including other subcellular structures.

gene_ontology.name: metal ion binding
gene_ontology.description: Binding to a metal ion.

gene_ontology.name: DNA binding
gene_ontology.description: Any molecular function by which a gene product interacts selectively and non-covalently with DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid).

gene_ontology.name: cell differentiation
gene_ontology.description: The cellular developmental process in which a relatively unspecialized cell, e.g. embryonic or regenerative cell, acquires specialized structural and/or functional features that characterize a specifi

gene_ontology.name: positive regulation of DNA-templated transcription
gene_ontology.description: Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of cellular DNA-templated transcription.

gene_ontology.name: RNA polymerase II cis-regulatory region sequence-specific DNA binding
gene_ontology.description: Binding to a specific upstream regulatory DNA sequence (transcription factor recognition sequence or binding site) located in cis relative to the transcription start site (i.e., on the same strand of

gene_ontology.name: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II
gene_ontology.description: Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of transcription mediated by RNA polymerase II.

gene_ontology.name: positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II
gene_ontology.description: Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of transcription from an RNA polymerase II promoter.

gene_ontology.name: sequence-specific DNA binding
gene_ontology.description: Binding to DNA of a specific nucleotide composition, e.g. GC-rich DNA binding, or with a specific sequence motif or type of DNA e.g. promotor binding or rDNA binding.

gene_ontology.name: negative regulation of DNA-templated transcription
gene_ontology.description: Any process that stops, prevents, or reduces the frequency, rate or extent of cellular DNA-templated transcription.

gene_ontology.name: negative regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II
gene_ontology.description: Any process that stops, prevents, or reduces the frequency, rate or extent of transcription mediated by RNA polymerase II.

gene_ontology.name: DNA-binding transcription activator activity
gene_ontology.description: GO_0001228", "A DNA-binding transcription factor activity that activates or increases transcription of specific gene sets transcribed by RNA polymerase II.

gene_ontology.name: transcription coregulator activity
gene_ontology.description: A transcription regulator activity that modulates the transcription of specific gene sets via binding to a DNA-binding transcription factor at a specific genomic locus, either on its own or as part of

gene_ontology.name: DNA-binding transcription repressor activity
gene_ontology.description: GO_0001227", "A DNA-binding transcription factor activity that represses or decreases the transcription of specific gene sets transcribed by RNA polymerase II.

gene_ontology.name: transcription coactivator activity
gene_ontology.description: A transcription coregulator activity that activates or increases the transcription of specific gene sets via binding to a DNA-binding transcription factor at a specific genomic locus, either on its ow

gene_ontology.name: positive regulation of cold-induced thermogenesis
gene_ontology.description: Any process that activates or increases the frequency, rate or extent of cold-induced thermogenesis.

gene_ontology.name: histone H3 methyltransferase activity
gene_ontology.description: Catalysis of the reaction: S-adenosyl-L-methionine + a histone H3 = S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + a methylated histone H3. Histone methylation generally occurs on either an arginine or a lysine residue.

gene_ontology.name: negative regulation of transforming growth factor beta receptor signaling pathway
gene_ontology.description: Any process that stops, prevents, or reduces the frequency, rate or extent of any TGF-beta receptor signaling pathway.

gene_ontology.name: DNA-binding transcription factor binding
gene_ontology.description: Binding to a DNA-binding transcription factor, a protein that interacts with a specific DNA sequence (sometimes referred to as a motif) within the regulatory region of a gene to modulate transcription

gene_ontology.name: transcription repressor complex
gene_ontology.description: A protein complex that possesses activity that prevents or downregulates transcription.

gene_ontology.name: heterochromatin organization
gene_ontology.description: Any process that results in the specification, formation or maintenance of the physical structure of eukaryotic heterochromatin, a compact and highly condensed form of chromatin.

gene_ontology.name: transcription cis-regulatory region binding

gene_ontology.name: brown fat cell differentiation
gene_ontology.description: The process in which a relatively unspecialized cell acquires specialized features of a brown adipocyte, an animal connective tissue cell involved in adaptive thermogenesis. Brown adipocytes contain m

gene_ontology.name: regulation of cellular respiration
gene_ontology.description: Any process that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of cellular respiration, the enzymatic release of energy from organic compounds.

gene_ontology.name: zinc ion binding
gene_ontology.description: Binding to a zinc ion (Zn).

gene_ontology.name: peroxisome organization
gene_ontology.description: A process that is carried out at the cellular level which results in the assembly, arrangement of constituent parts, or disassembly of a peroxisome. A peroxisome is a small, membrane-bounded organelle

gene_ontology.name: peroxisomal membrane
gene_ontology.description: The lipid bilayer surrounding a peroxisome.

gene_ontology.name: protein import into peroxisome matrix

gene_ontology.name: membrane
gene_ontology.description: A lipid bilayer along with all the proteins and protein complexes embedded in it and attached to it.

gene_ontology.name: transferase activity
gene_ontology.description: Catalysis of the transfer of a group, e.g. a methyl group, glycosyl group, acyl group, phosphorus-containing, or other groups, from one compound (generally regarded as the donor) to another compound (

gene_ontology.name: protein ubiquitination
gene_ontology.description: The process in which one or more ubiquitin groups are added to a protein.

gene_ontology.name: protein transport
gene_ontology.description: The directed movement of proteins into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore.

gene_ontology.name: peroxisome
gene_ontology.description: A small organelle enclosed by a single membrane, and found in most eukaryotic cells. Contains peroxidases and other enzymes involved in a variety of metabolic processes including free radical detoxifi

gene_ontology.name: ubiquitin protein ligase activity
gene_ontology.description: Catalysis of the transfer of ubiquitin to a substrate protein via the reaction X-ubiquitin + S = X + S-ubiquitin, where X is either an E2 or E3 enzyme, the X-ubiquitin linkage is a thioester bond, and

gene_ontology.name: protein polyubiquitination
gene_ontology.description: Addition of multiple ubiquitin groups to a protein, forming a ubiquitin chain.

gene_ontology.name: proteasome-mediated ubiquitin-dependent protein catabolic process
gene_ontology.description: The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the breakdown of a protein or peptide by hydrolysis of its peptide bonds, initiated by the covalent attachment of ubiquitin, and mediated by the protea

gene_ontology.name: protein quality control for misfolded or incompletely synthesized proteins
gene_ontology.description: The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the breakdown of misfolded or attenuated proteins.

anatomical_structures.name: Acinar Cell
anatomical_structures.definition: A secretory cell found in pancreatic acini that secretes digestive enzymes and mucins. This cell is a typical zymogenic cell, have a basal nucleus and basophilic cytoplasm consisting of regular arrays

anatomical_structures.name: Stellate Cell
anatomical_structures.definition: A cell that is found in the periacinar space of the exocrine pancreas and in perivascular and periductal regions of the pancreas, and has long cytoplasmic processes that encircle the base of the acinu

anatomical_structures.name: Alpha Cell
anatomical_structures.definition: A type A enteroendocrine cell found in the periphery of the islets of Langerhans that secretes glucagon.

anatomical_structures.name: Beta Cell
anatomical_structures.definition: A cell that secretes insulin and is located towards the center of the islets of Langerhans.

anatomical_structures.name: Delta Cell
anatomical_structures.definition: A D cell located in the pancreas. Peripherally placed within the islets like type A cells; contains somatostatin.

anatomical_structures.name: Ductal Cell
anatomical_structures.definition: Epithelial cell found in the ducts of the pancreas. This cell type contributes to the high luminal pH.

anatomical_structures.name: Endothelial Cell
anatomical_structures.definition: An endothelial cell comprises the outermost layer or lining of anatomical structures and can be squamous or cuboidal. In mammals, endothelial cell has vimentin filaments and is derived from the mesode

anatomical_structures.name: Macrophage Cell
